html:
[633]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 亦太剥人面皮 - Literal meaning 'also too peels off human skin'; idiom meaning 'also too harsh' or 'also too severe'.
  • 何竟咆哮于此 - Meaning 'Why are you roaring here?'
  • 龚犹未解 - Meaning 'Gong still did not understand'.
  • 俄闻虎啸 - Meaning 'Soon after, he heard a tiger roar'.
  • 声震山谷 - Meaning 'The sound shook the mountains and valleys'.
  • 龚乃大惊 - Meaning 'Gong was greatly surprised'.
  • 几坠崖下 - Meaning 'Almost fell off the cliff'.
  • 镜儿掖之曰 - Meaning 'Mirror held him and said'.
  • 有妾在，何震恐乃尔 - Meaning 'With me here, why are you so frightened?'
  • 乃呼曰 - Meaning 'Then she called out'.
  • 儿与婿将他适 - Meaning 'My child and her husband will go elsewhere'.
  • 承母玉成 - Meaning 'Thanks to your mother's help'.
  • 异日归当效反哺 - Meaning 'On the day they return, they will repay the favor like a bird feeding its parents'.
  • 言未已 - Meaning 'Before she finished speaking'.
  • 虎忽潜踪 - Meaning 'The tiger suddenly disappeared'.
  • 龚心少定 - Meaning 'Gong's heart calmed slightly'.
  • 反戏曰 - Meaning 'Then he joked'.
  • 昔假虎威 - Meaning 'In the past, I pretended to have the tiger's power'.
  • 今日威可伏虎矣 - Meaning 'Today, my power can subdue the tiger'.
  • 及抵山麓 - Meaning 'When they reached the foot of the mountain'.
  • 有村落 - Meaning 'There was a village'.
  • 暂假朝饔 - Meaning 'They temporarily borrowed breakfast'.
  • 遂显称夫妇 - Meaning 'They then openly called themselves husband and wife'.
  • 人亦无自究诘 - Meaning 'No one questioned them'.
  • 镜儿又出白金觅代步 - Meaning 'Mirror then took out white gold to find a means of transportation'.
  • 置衣装 - Meaning 'They bought clothes and other items'.
  • 竟纡道由京而南 - Meaning 'They took a detour from the capital to the south'.
  • 直抵通郡 - Meaning 'Directly to Tong County'.
  • 不日解缆登舟 - Meaning 'In no time, they untied the boat and set sail'.
  • 一帆南去 - Meaning 'They sailed south with one sail'.
  • 迨其季父之召来 - Meaning 'By the time his uncle called for him'.
  • 龚早出山三日矣 - Meaning 'Gong had already left the mountain three days earlier'.
  • 舟中龚与镜儿志始得畅 - Meaning 'On the boat, Gong and Mirror finally felt at ease'.
  • 或涤瓯茗战 - Meaning 'Sometimes they washed cups and played chess with tea'.
  • 或剪烛手谈 - Meaning 'Sometimes they trimmed candles and played chess by candlelight'.
  • 或举诗书为觞政 - Meaning 'Sometimes they used poetry and books as a drinking game'.
  • 或纪风景于行笥 - Meaning 'Sometimes they recorded the scenery in their travel cases'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 虎啸 - Refers to the roar of a tiger, often used in literature to create a sense of danger or awe.
  • 玉成 - A term used to describe someone who helps bring about a successful outcome, often translated as 'to help bring about a favorable result'.
  • 反哺 - A metaphor for filial piety, where young birds feed their parents, symbolizing gratitude and repayment of favors.
  • 白金 - Refers to silver, often used as a form of currency in ancient times.
  • 代步 - Means 'means of transportation,' often used to describe vehicles or other forms of travel.
  • 纡道 - Means 'to take a detour' or 'to go a roundabout way'.
  • 京 - Refers to the capital city, often used to denote the political center of a region.
  • 通郡 - Refers to a specific place, Tong County, which was likely an important location in the story.
  • 季父 - Refers to an uncle, specifically the younger brother of one's father.

Proper Nouns

  • 龚 - Refers to Gong, the main male character.
  • 镜儿 - Refers to Mirror, the main female character.
  • 季父 - Refers to Gong's uncle.
  • 通郡 - Refers to Tong County, a specific location in the story.

[634]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 分题限韵，更唱迭和 - Poetic term meaning 'dividing topics and limiting rhymes, alternating verses and responses,' describing a collaborative poetic process.
  • 豪放 - Literally 'bold and unrestrained,' referring to a free-spirited and expressive style of poetry.
  • 晦明无以异焉 - Phrase meaning 'there is no difference between brightness and darkness,' suggesting consistency regardless of external conditions.
  • 布帆乃能无恙 - Metaphorically means 'only when a person of great fortune arrives can the journey proceed without danger.'
  • 巨浪坌起，阴霾匝地 - Describes a scene of sudden, overwhelming waves and dark clouds covering the sky, symbolizing impending danger or chaos.
  • 留五日 - Literally 'staying for five days,' indicating a period of waiting or delay.
  • 大德者 - Refers to someone of great virtue or moral standing.
  • 厚福者 - Refers to someone of great fortune or good luck.
  • 共济 - Means 'to share a boat' or 'to face difficulties together,' symbolizing mutual support and cooperation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伍相范伯 - Refers to Wu Zixu and Fan Li, historical figures from the Spring and Autumn period, associated with the Jiang River and revered as deities in local folklore.
  • 江神 - Refers to river deities, particularly those associated with the Jiang River, often seen as powerful and unpredictable.
  • 扁舟 - Literally 'a small boat,' often used in classical literature to symbolize simplicity, freedom, or a solitary journey.

Proper Nouns

  • 龚 - The name of the protagonist, Gong.
  • 镜儿 - The name of the female character, Jing'er.
  • 伍相范伯 - Historical figures Wu Zixu and Fan Li.
  • 江神 - River deities associated with the Jiang River.
  • 扁舟 - A small boat, often used in classical literature.

[635]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 然自此鏡兒不復晝見 - A narrative transition indicating that from this point on, the mirror no longer appears during the day.
  • 此人孝子，諸天擁護 - A statement that this person is a filial son and is protected by the heavens.
  • 妾若肆然無忌，恐有不虞，當謹避之 - An expression of caution, meaning 'if I were to act recklessly without restraint, I fear there might be unforeseen consequences, so I should be cautious and avoid it.'
  • 故續與龔共載一舟，究不知其攜眷而遊也 - A narrative statement that 'therefore, the ghost continued to share a boat with Gong, though it was unknown whether she was traveling with her family.'
  • 比入大江之中，波平浪靜，如履康莊 - A metaphorical description of the journey, meaning 'when they entered the great river, the waves were calm and the journey was as smooth as traveling on a broad, level road.'
  • 數日遂抵杭 - A simple statement indicating that after several days, they arrived at Hangzhou.
  • 妾至貴邑，土神尚慮不容，得師一言，為吾緩頰，庶可與子偕老 - A plea for help, meaning 'I am traveling to your prestigious city, and even the local deities may not accept me. If you can speak on my behalf to the master, perhaps we can live together until old age.'
  • 師早知之，龔固不勝惶悚 - A description of the master's foreknowledge and Gong's anxiety, meaning 'the master had already known this, and Gong was deeply anxious.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鏡兒 - Refers to a ghost or spirit, specifically a female one, who is involved in the narrative.
  • 孝子 - Refers to a filial son, a concept deeply rooted in Confucian ethics.
  • 諸天 - Refers to the heavens or celestial deities, indicating divine protection.
  • 續 - Refers to the ghost, who continues to interact with Gong.
  • 龔 - Refers to the human protagonist, Gong, who is the focus of the narrative.
  • 貴邑 - Refers to a prestigious city, likely Hangzhou, which is mentioned as the destination.
  • 土神 - Refers to local deities or earth spirits, who may have jurisdiction over the area.
  • 師 - Refers to a master or spiritual authority, likely a Buddhist monk or a respected figure.
  • 勘合 - Refers to an official permit or document, often used in historical contexts to signify authority or permission.

Proper Nouns

  • 鏡兒 - The ghost or spirit, referred to as 'mirror girl' or 'mirror spirit.'
  • 龔 - The human protagonist, Gong.
  • 師 - The master or spiritual authority, likely a Buddhist monk or a respected figure.
  • 貴邑 - The prestigious city, likely Hangzhou.
  • 土神 - Local deities or earth spirits.
  • 勘合 - An official permit or document.

[636]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 亟與鏡兒歸舟 - 急切地与镜儿一同返回舟中
  • 果無扞格 - 果然没有障碍或隔阂
  • 托言季父為娶於京 - 假托言辞说小叔子在京都为他娶妻
  • 戚里莫不信之 - 亲戚们没有不信的
  • 龚故早失怙恃 - 龚早年失去父母
  • 以镜儿主内政 - 由镜儿主持家务
  • 颇能持家 - 相当能够管理家务
  • 且出金钱数万 - 并且拿出数万金钱
  • 为营田宅 - 购置田宅
  • 龚以骤富 - 龚因此突然富有
  • 向未见其携一文 - 过去从未见过他带一分钱
  • 今乃取之不竭 - 现在竟然取之不尽
  • 亦可异已 - 也非常奇怪
  • 薄暮始抵龚处 - 傍晚才到达龚处
  • 则门庭巍焕 - 门庭高大辉煌
  • 俨然素封 - 俨然如同没有官职但富有
  • 揖续入 - 作揖继续进入
  • 盛筵相款 - 丰盛的宴席款待
  • 镜儿所诞之子 - 镜儿所生的儿子
  • 岁已三周 - 已经三岁
  • 眉目之秀丽 - 眉毛和眼睛的秀丽
  • 迥异恒儿 - 非常不同于普通孩子
  • 由是可想见其母 - 由此可以想见其母亲
  • 饮至夜分 - 饮至半夜
  • 龚始别去 - 龚才告别离开
  • 供帐之丰美 - 招待用的帷帐丰盛美好
  • 益不待言 - 更加不言自明
  • 翌日辞行 - 第二天告别出发
  • 龚亦不再援止 - 龚也不再挽留
  • 惟曰 - 只说
  • 道路辽阔 - 道路广阔
  • 尊翁恐不克相值 - 尊贵的长者恐怕无法相遇
  • 还时祈再过我一叙 - 回来时请求再来看我叙谈
  • 续唯唯 - 续唯唯应声附和

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 龚 - 指龚姓，具体人物不详
  • 镜儿 - 指龚的伴侣或妻子
  • 季父 - 指小叔子或叔父
  • 京 - 指京城，具体地点不详
  • 戚里 - 指亲戚
  • 怙恃 - 指父母
  • 内政 - 指家务
  • 骤富 - 指突然富有
  • 素封 - 指没有官职但富有
  • 夜分 - 指半夜
  • 供帐 - 指招待用的帷帐
  • 尊翁 - 指尊贵的长者

Proper Nouns

  • 龚 - 指龚姓，具体人物不详
  • 镜儿 - 指龚的伴侣或妻子
  • 季父 - 指小叔子或叔父
  • 京 - 指京城，具体地点不详
  • 戚里 - 指亲戚
  • 怙恃 - 指父母
  • 内政 - 指家务
  • 骤富 - 指突然富有
  • 素封 - 指没有官职但富有
  • 夜分 - 指半夜
  • 供帐 - 指招待用的帷帐
  • 尊翁 - 指尊贵的长者

[637]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 悒怏 - Compound word meaning 'disappointed' or 'depressed,' often used to describe a state of mind.
  • 遄归 - Means 'to return quickly' or 'to hasten back.'
  • 抱终天之恨 - Phrase meaning 'to carry a lifelong regret' or 'to bear eternal remorse.'
  • 先见 - Refers to foresight or prescience.
  • 优游畎亩 - Literally means 'to leisurely live in the fields,' but conveys the idea of living a peaceful and contented life in rural simplicity.
  • 杳然 - Means 'mysterious' or 'unknown,' often used to describe something that is untraceable or lost.
  • 雪山成道 - Refers to achieving enlightenment on Mount Snow (or a similar mountain), often used in Buddhist contexts.
  • 忉利之天 - Refers to the Trāyastriṃśa Heaven, a level of heaven in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 人生一大憾事 - Means 'a great regret in life' or 'a significant disappointment in life.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 龚 - Likely a proper name, possibly referring to a historical figure or a character in a literary work.
  • 镜儿 - Another proper name, likely referring to a character in the story.
  • 雷峰塔 - A famous Buddhist pagoda in Hangzhou, China, often featured in Chinese literature and folklore.
  • 法海老髡 - Refers to Venerable Faha, a monk in the legend of the White Snake, known for his role in sealing the White Snake under the Leifeng Pagoda.
  • 儒 - Refers to Confucianism, one of the major philosophical and ethical traditions in Chinese culture.
  • 恻隐仁恕 - Terms from Confucian ethics, referring to compassion, benevolence, and forgiveness.

Proper Nouns

  • 龚 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure or a character in a literary work.
  • 镜儿 - A proper name, likely referring to a character in the story.
  • 雷峰塔 - The Leifeng Pagoda, a famous Buddhist pagoda in Hangzhou, China.
  • 法海老髡 - Venerable Faha, a monk in the legend of the White Snake.
  • 儒 - Confucianism, one of the major philosophical and ethical traditions in Chinese culture.
  • 雪山 - Mount Snow, a place often associated with Buddhist enlightenment.
  • 忉利之天 - The Trāyastriṃśa Heaven, a level of heaven in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 本一 - A proper name, likely referring to a character in the story.

[638]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 随园老人曰 - Conventional opening formula meaning 'the old man of the Su Yuan said,' used to introduce the speaker or narrator.
  • 两事迥不相谋 - Means 'two matters that are completely unrelated,' where '迥不相谋' is a classical idiom indicating a lack of connection or relationship.
  • 而合成一片 - Means 'yet they form a single entity,' suggesting an unexpected unity despite the lack of apparent connection.
  • 几如无缝天衣 - A metaphor meaning 'almost like a seamless robe,' which is a classical idiom often used to describe something that is perfectly integrated or harmonious.
  • 旖旎文字 - Means 'graceful and beautiful writing,' where '旖旎' is an archaic term for elegance and charm.
  • 不独奇事 - Means 'not just a strange event,' indicating that there is more to the story than just the unusual nature of the event itself.
  • 翠微娘子 - A proper name, likely referring to a specific person in the narrative.
  • 授室 - Means 'to give a wife,' indicating marriage.
  • 孑然 - Means 'alone' or 'solitary.'
  • 纳妇言 - Means 'to accept the advice of his wife,' indicating a decision influenced by his wife's words.
  • 斥为傲弟 - Means 'to scold as an arrogant younger brother,' indicating a harsh judgment.
  • 笞之十数 - Means 'to flog him several times,' describing a form of corporal punishment.
  • 夜挟白刃 - Means 'at night with a sharp blade,' indicating the preparation for violence.
  • 行及闾侧 - Means 'walking up to the gate of the neighborhood,' indicating the location where the father is encountered.
  • 大恸 - Means 'to weep bitterly,' indicating intense grief.
  • 策杖而来 - Means 'to come with a walking stick,' indicating the father's appearance.
  • 厉声呵之 - Means 'to scold him loudly,' indicating the father's stern reaction.
  • 畜产意欲何为 - Means 'what do you, a lowly creature, intend to do?' indicating a derogatory and commanding tone.
  • 岂丈夫竟不克自立耶 - Means 'how can a man not be able to stand on his own?' indicating a challenge to the son's manhood.
  • 幸有薄积 - Means 'fortunately, I have a small savings,' indicating the father's modest financial situation.
  • 阋墙至此 - Means 'to quarrel like this within the family,' indicating internal family conflict.
  • 倘无立锥 - Means 'if we had no place to stand,' indicating extreme poverty.
  • 若又将如何 - Means 'what would you do then?' indicating a rhetorical question about future consequences.
  • 抚其背 - Means 'to pat his back,' indicating a gesture of comfort or reassurance.
  • 挥泪而归 - Means 'to return with tears,' indicating a sad but determined departure.
  • 不再做拚生之想 - Means 'no longer to think of dying,' indicating a change in the son's mindset.
  • 明日即襆被而行 - Means 'the next day, he packed his bedding and left,' indicating a sudden and decisive action.
  • 亦不往辞兄嫂 - Means 'he also did not bid farewell to his brother and sister-in-law,' indicating a lack of communication before leaving.
  • 即兄嫂亦不意其有所之 - Means 'even his brother and sister-in-law did not expect him to go anywhere,' indicating their surprise at the son's departure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 随园老人 - The narrator, likely a respected figure in the community, using the name 'Su Yuan Old Man' to establish authority.
  • 翠微娘子 - A specific person in the narrative, possibly a distant relative or acquaintance who can provide support.
  • 授室 - The act of giving a wife, a traditional practice in Chinese culture.
  • 纳妇言 - The influence of a wife's advice, reflecting the role of women in family decisions.
  • 笞之十数 - A form of corporal punishment, reflecting the legal and social norms of the time.
  • 夜挟白刃 - The act of carrying a weapon at night, indicating the severity of the situation and the potential for violence.
  • 策杖而来 - The image of an elderly person coming with a walking stick, reflecting the respect for older generations.
  • 厉声呵之 - The stern command, reflecting the strict and authoritative nature of the father.
  • 畜产 - A derogatory term for a lowly creature, reflecting the father's frustration and disappointment.
  • 幸有薄积 - The modest savings, reflecting the family's economic status.
  • 阋墙 - The internal family conflict, reflecting the breakdown of family harmony.
  • 倘无立锥 - The extreme poverty, reflecting the dire circumstances the family might face.
  • 若又将如何 - The rhetorical question about future consequences, reflecting the father's concern for the son's well-being.
  • 挥泪而归 - The sad but determined departure, reflecting the son's emotional state and decision.
  • 不再做拚生之想 - The change in the son's mindset, reflecting his decision to avoid violence and seek a better path.

Proper Nouns

  • 随园老人 - The narrator, likely a respected figure in the community.
  • 翠微娘子 - A specific person in the narrative, possibly a distant relative or acquaintance.
  • 甲 - The elder son, who has already married.
  • 乙 - The younger son, who is still single and faces conflict with his brother.
  • 跛叟 - The old man with a limp, known for his medical skills.

[639]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乙行数日 - Means 'After several days of travel,' where '乙' is a placeholder name for the protagonist.
  • 咸不识翠微娘子为谁 - Means 'Everyone did not know who the Lady Cuiwei was.'
  • 乙亦自谓父诳己 - Means 'He also believed his father had deceived him.'
  • 天下必无是人 - Means 'There must be no such person in the world.'
  • 遂止于旅邸 - Means 'So he stopped at an inn.'
  • 不复前 - Means 'and did not continue forward.'
  • 资斧告竭 - Means 'His funds were exhausted.'
  • 进退维谷 - Means 'He was in a difficult situation, unable to advance or retreat.'
  • 正仓皇间 - Means 'In the midst of his distress.'
  • 闻旅人相语曰 - Means 'He heard travelers talking to each other, saying.'
  • 明夕宿处，当有剧可观 - Means 'Tomorrow night's lodging place will have something worth seeing.'
  • 盍遄行 - Means 'Why not hurry on?'
  • 因盛称其声容 - Means 'They highly praised its appearance and performance.'
  • 洋洋盈耳 - Means 'It filled the ears with sound.'
  • 乙时方抱闷 - Means 'He was feeling depressed at the time.'
  • 顿思一往 - Means 'Suddenly thought of going.'
  • 以畅郁怀 - Means 'To relieve his inner frustration.'
  • 乃复早旦启行 - Means 'So he set out again early the next morning.'
  • 跋涉又将百里 - Means 'He traveled another hundred miles.'
  • 及至其处 - Means 'When he arrived there.'
  • 天尚未暝 - Means 'The sky had not yet darkened.'
  • 亦不谋所栖止 - Means 'He also did not plan where to stay.'
  • 先往观焉 - Means 'He first went to watch.'
  • 适演《千金记》 - Means 'Just then, they were performing the *Qianjin Ji*.'
  • 霸王挥戈 - Means 'The Overlord (Xiang Yu) was wielding his halberd.'
  • 韩侯命将 - Means 'The Marquis of Han (Han Xin) was commanding his generals.'
  • 场上几无隙地 - Means 'There was hardly any empty space on the stage.'
  • 而钲鼓雷鸣 - Means 'And the drums and gongs sounded like thunder.'
  • 敖曹聒耳 - Means 'The loud noise was deafening.'
  • 颇可泄人幽郁 - Means 'It was quite good at relieving one's inner frustration.'
  • 乙鹄立众中 - Means 'He stood like a crane among the crowd.'
  • 看至终场 - Means 'He watched until the end of the performance.'
  • 甫思往觅邮亭 - Means 'Just as he thought of going to find a post station.'
  • 忽一人揖而语之曰 - Means 'Suddenly, a person bowed and said to him.'
  • 郎非跛翁之子乎 - Means 'Are you not the son of the lame old man?'
  • 翠微娘子固命予相俟久矣 - Means 'Lady Cuiwei has indeed ordered me to wait for you for a long time.'
  • 视其人 - Means 'He looked at the person.'
  • 青衣矮帽 - Means 'Wearing a blue robe and a short hat.'
  • 状若仆人 - Means 'He looked like a servant.'
  • 亦不暇细询 - Means 'He also did not have time to ask in detail.'
  • 惟诘曰 - Means 'But he asked.'
  • 娘子安在 - Means 'Where is Lady Cuiwei?'
  • 予实奉老父命敬来晋谒者 - Means 'I am here to respectfully pay my respects on behalf of my old father.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 翠微娘子 - A reference to a character or person known as 'Lady Cuiwei.' The exact identity is not specified, but it suggests a person of some importance or reputation.
  • 跛翁 - Refers to a lame old man, possibly a figure of authority or a father figure in the story.
  • 《千金记》 - Likely refers to a play or performance, possibly a well-known dramatic work.
  • 霸王 - Refers to Xiang Yu, a famous historical figure known as the Overlord during the Chu-Han Contention.
  • 韩侯 - Refers to Han Xin, a famous general during the same period.
  • 邮亭 - Refers to a post station or inn where travelers could rest and send messages.

Proper Nouns

  • 乙 - The protagonist, a placeholder name.
  • 翠微娘子 - A character or person of some importance.
  • 跛翁 - A lame old man, possibly a father figure.
  • 《千金记》 - A play or performance.
  • 霸王 - Xiang Yu, a historical figure.
  • 韩侯 - Han Xin, a historical figure.
  • 邮亭 - A post station or inn.

[640]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其人亦大悦 - Common expression meaning 'the person was greatly pleased'.
  • 请与偕行 - Means 'asked to accompany him'.
  • 由村后约行半里许 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'after traveling about half a li from the village'.
  • 别有巨宅，闬闳高峻，栋宇巍峨 - Vivid description of a large, impressive mansion.
  • 门外立健卒十余，皆披坚执锐，环立森严 - Describes the scene outside the mansion with armed guards standing in a strict formation.
  • 又有小卤簿，幢幡杂色，云軿攒花 - Refers to a small entourage with colorful flags and decorated carriages.
  • 咸云娘子将往赴盛会 - Means 'all said that the lady was going to attend a grand gathering'.
  • 乙遂震竦不敢前 - Describes the reaction of the person (乙) who became frightened and dared not proceed.

  • 藉主人庇荫，亦无敢言者 - A compound sentence where '藉主人庇荫' (under the protection of the master) is the condition, and '亦无敢言者' (no one dared to speak) is the result.
  • 明年，孝廉将赴公车，以诸仆为无用，独携吴与二僮行 - A compound sentence where '明年' (the next year) is the time frame, '孝廉将赴公车' (the scholar was about to go to the capital) is the main action, '以诸仆为无用' (considering all the servants useless) is the reason, and '独携吴与二僮行' (only took Wu and two boys with him) is the action.
  • 朱提论千，綵缯无算，皆付吴以为心膂 - A compound sentence where '朱提论千' (thousands of zhu coins) and '綵缯无算' (countless colorful silks) are the items, and '皆付吴以为心膂' (all given to Wu to be his trusted companion) is the action.

  • 行未二日程，至某县之野，林深地僻，行旅绝踪 - A compound sentence where '行未二日程' (after traveling for two days), '至某县之野' (arrived at a place in a certain county), '林深地僻' (the forest was deep and the place was remote), and '行旅绝踪' (no travelers were seen) describe the setting.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '询之，女亦腼然不答' describes the reaction of the woman when asked about her mother, indicating her shyness or embarrassment.
  • The sentence '数月后，偶过邻邑，遇一顾姓，间以女母诘之，并举女之乳名，愕然曰' describes the encounter with a man from the same surname and the revelation about the woman's mother.
  • The sentence '孝廉见其吻合，乃大喜' describes the scholar's reaction upon realizing the truth.
  • The sentence '要以至家，使以兄妹礼见女，女亦不拒' describes the invitation to the scholar's home and the meeting between the scholar and the woman.
  • The sentence '阿惜自是始识父家，时一归宁，两姓竟成姻戚' describes the relationship between the scholar and the woman, including their eventual marriage.
  • The sentence '乃孝廉自遭三败，壮心顿灰，不复干预人事' describes the scholar's change in attitude after experiencing three failures.
  • The sentence '人亦知其室有剑仙，惧不再逞' describes the people's fear of the scholar's supernatural abilities.
  • The sentence '此故明天启五年事也' specifies the time period of the story.
  • The sentence '国初，犹须发皓然，而精神矍铄，每语人曰' describes the scholar's appearance and his advice to others.

  • 蒙不洁 - Means 'to be defiled' or 'to be impure.'

  • 阳明正直之神 - Refers to the pure and upright deities.
  • 屏息而避之 - Means 'to hold one's breath and avoid it,' indicating a strong aversion or fear.

  • 昔京西有异僧 - 'In the past, there was a strange monk in the western part of the capital.'
  • 斫二石球 - 'Cut two stone balls,' referring to the creation of stone balls by the monk.

  • 已乃下而掇石，再登再推，操纵终日 - 'Then he would descend, pick up the stones, climb again, and push them again, controlling them all day long.'
  • 人因号之曰魔 - 'People therefore called him a demon,' indicating the monk's unconventional and possibly unsettling behavior.
  • 而钦其道行者来且接踵 - 'And those who admired his spiritual practice came in a continuous stream.'
  • 僧于丈夫犹见礼，独至巾帼者流则谩骂曰 - 'The monk treated men with respect, but when it came to women, he would curse them, saying...' indicating the monk's different treatment of men and women.
  • 没家法！露面出头，直供妄男子看杀耳，岂真为老僧哉！ - 'No family discipline! Showing your face and head, you are just for the sake of being looked at by foolish men, not truly for the old monk!' The monk's curse criticizing the lack of proper conduct among women.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 盗为之动容 - Indicates that the thief was moved or impressed by something.
  • 弟少不屑，早背椿萱 - Classical expression where '椿萱' (chūn xuān) is a metonym for parents. '早背椿萱' means 'early left parents' or 'lost parents at a young age.'
  • 不知有姊，非敢恝然相忘 - '恝然' (jiárán) means 'indifferently' or 'without care.' The phrase suggests that the thief did not know he had an older sister and did not dare to forget her indifferently.
  • 媪又曰 - '媪' (ǎo) refers to an elderly woman.
  • 弟幼时予犹归宁 - '归宁' (guīníng) means 'returning to one's parents' home,' often used to describe a daughter-in-law visiting her in-laws.
  • 后从汝姊夫远出至此 - '姊夫' (zǐfū) means 'sister's husband' or 'brother-in-law.'
  • 遂不得返 - Means 'thus could not return.'
  • 岂意怙恃皆弃弟仙逝 - '怙恃' (hùshì) means 'parents' or 'parental figures.' '仙逝' (xiānshì) is a euphemism for 'passed away.'
  • 弟已成立如此耶 - '成立' (chénglì) means 'grown up' or 'established.'
  • 言已大恸 - '大恸' (dàtòng) means 'great sorrow' or 'deep grief.'
  • 盗竟信为实然 - '竟' (jìng) means 'finally' or 'indeed.' '实然' (shírán) means 'as it really is.'
  • 劝慰至再 - '至再' (zhìzài) means 'repeatedly' or 'twice.'
  • 随媪入室 - '随' (suí) means 'followed.' '入室' (rùshì) means 'into the room.'
  • 媪命婢烛 - '命' (mìng) means 'ordered.' '婢' (bì) means 'maid.' '烛' (zhú) means 'lit the candle.'
  • 盗复出约束其众曰 - '复出' (fùchū) means 'went out again.' '约束' (yuēshù) means 'to restrain' or 'to discipline.' '其众' (qízhòng) means 'his followers.'
  • 此予之姊家，非路人也，慎勿骚扰 - '予之姊家' (yǔzhīzǐjiā) means 'my sister's home.' '非路人也' (fēilùrén yě) means 'not a stranger.' '慎勿骚扰' (shènwù sāorǎo) means 'be careful not to disturb.'
  • 媪心窃喜 - '心窃喜' (xīn qiè xǐ) means 'secretly happy.'
  • 又呼其子出见舅 - '呼' (hū) means 'called.' '其子' (qízǐ) means 'his son.' '见舅' (jiànjiù) means 'to meet his uncle.'
  • 其子知为盗，举股栗 - '知为盗' (zhī wéi dào) means 'knew he was a thief.' '举股栗' (jǔgǔlì) means 'trembled all over.'
  • 不得已而参谒之 - '不得已' (bùdéyǐ) means 'had no choice.' '参谒' (cānyè) means 'pay respects to.'
  • 甥皆长成，姊真厚福人也 - '甥' (shēng) means 'nephew' or 'niece.' '皆长成' (jiē chángchéng) means 'all grown up.' '姊真厚福人也' (zǐ zhēn hòufú rén yě) means 'my sister is truly a person of great fortune.'
  • 媪又命子妇出参舅翁 - '子妇' (zǐfù) means 'daughter-in-law.' '舅翁' (jiùwēng) means 'uncle-in-law.'
  • 时媪之中子方授室 - '中子' (zhōngzǐ) means 'middle son.' '方授室' (fāng shòushì) means 'was just married.'
  • 新妆出拜，恍若一家人 - '新妆' (xīnzhuāng) means 'newly dressed.' '恍若一家人' (huǎngruò yījiārén) means 'seemed like one family.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 盗 - Refers to a thief or bandit.
  • 椿萱 - A metonym for parents, often used in classical literature.
  • 归宁 - The act of a daughter-in-law visiting her in-laws, a common practice in traditional Chinese society.
  • 姊夫 - The brother-in-law of the old woman, who is the thief's sister's husband.
  • 仙逝 - A euphemism for death, often used in classical Chinese to show respect.
  • 约束其众 - The act of disciplining or controlling one's followers, a common theme in stories of bandits and outlaws.
  • 参谒 - The act of paying respects, often used in formal or familial contexts.
  • 授室 - The act of getting married, specifically for a man.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence structure indicates that the thief was moved by something and then spoke.
  • This phrase is elliptical, meaning the thief left his parents at a young age.
  • The sentence structure suggests that the thief did not know he had an older sister and did not dare to forget her indifferently.
  • The sentence structure indicates that the old woman spoke again.
  • This sentence is a sequence of events, indicating that the old woman was young when she visited her in-laws and then left with her brother-in-law, never to return.
  • The sentence structure suggests that the old woman did not expect her parents to die and that her brother had grown up.
  • The sentence structure indicates that the old woman spoke and then wept greatly.
  • The sentence structure indicates that the thief believed the old woman's story, comforted her, and then followed her into the room.
  • The sentence structure indicates that the old woman ordered the maid to light the candle and then the thief went out again to discipline his followers.
  • The sentence structure indicates that the thief told his followers that this was his sister's home and to be careful not to disturb anyone.
  • The sentence structure indicates that the old woman was secretly happy and then called her son to meet his uncle.
  • The sentence structure indicates that the son knew the thief was a bandit, trembled, and had no choice but to pay respects.
  • The sentence structure indicates that the thief's nephews were all grown up and that his sister was truly a person of great fortune.
  • The sentence structure indicates that the old woman then called her daughter-in-law to pay respects to her uncle-in-law, who was just married and newly dressed, making them seem like one family.

  • 今其人犹在故乡 - '今' means 'now,' '其人' means 'that person,' '犹' means 'still,' and '故乡' means 'hometown.' This phrase indicates that the person is still in his hometown.
  • 年仅廿五六 - '年' means 'age,' '仅' means 'only,' and '廿五六' means 'twenty-five or twenty-six.' This phrase suggests that the person is only twenty-five or twenty-six years old.
  • 而据事论断，老吏弗如 - '而' means 'but,' '据事论断' means 'to judge based on facts,' and '老吏弗如' means 'older officials are not as good.' This phrase suggests that despite his young age, the judge is better at judging than older officials.
  • 他年重履琴堂 - '他年' means 'in the future,' '重履' means 'to step on again,' and '琴堂' means 'the judge's office.' This phrase suggests that in the future, the judge will return to his office.

  • 晨起將見杖者陳謝 - At dawn, about to meet the cane-bearer to apologize. This phrase combines a time reference with an action and a social custom.
  • 然後辭歸 - Then bid farewell and return. This is a formal expression indicating a departure after a meeting or event.
  • 且少露求耦之意 - And slightly reveal the intention of seeking a partner. This phrase suggests a subtle hint at marriage intentions.
  • 忽女郎肌容憔悴，神色凄惶 - Suddenly, the young lady's appearance was emaciated, and her expression was sorrowful and anxious. This describes a sudden change in appearance and demeanor.
  • 疾趨而来 - Rushed over. This indicates a hurried and urgent approach.
  • 妾以衵衣在君处，势难挽回 - I have my inner garments in your possession, and it is difficult to retrieve them. This refers to a situation where the speaker has personal items in the possession of another, making it hard to take back.
  • 不得已而告父，以冀俯从 - Out of necessity, I informed my father, hoping for his approval. This shows a desperate attempt to gain parental consent.
  • 不意家严震怒，大奋雷霆 - Unexpectedly, my father was furious, and he flew into a rage.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 于路忽自计曰 - On the road, he suddenly thought to himself; indicates a moment of introspection.
  • 陆女既已纳聘，且命于亲 - Lu's daughter has already been betrothed, and the marriage is arranged by the parents; reflects traditional arranged marriages.
  • 此女未禀高堂 - This girl has not yet been approved by her parents; emphasizes the importance of parental consent.
  • 前盟不可寒，后约必不能践 - The previous promise cannot be broken, but the new one will surely not be fulfilled; highlights the dilemma of conflicting commitments.
  • 王魁李益之事，将见于予身 - The fate of Wang Kui and Li Yi will befall me; references historical figures known for their tragic love stories.
  • 其若之何 - What should I do about it; expresses a sense of helplessness.
  • 宝念及此，心始踌躇 - Bao thought of this and hesitated; shows internal conflict.
  • 行且至家，陡生谲计 - As he was about to return home, he suddenly devised a cunning plan; indicates a shift in strategy.
  • 旧姻若就，新特难求 - If the old marriage is consummated, the new one will be hard to find; reflects the difficulty of changing one's marital status.
  • 倘失此佳人，不如死 - If I lose this beautiful woman, I might as well die; expresses the depth of his feelings.
  • 吾闻父母将诹日为予毕姻 - I heard that my parents are going to choose a date to finalize the marriage; indicates the parents' role in arranging the marriage.
  • 盍重赂日者，诡称陆女之年庚实于翁姑不利 - Why not bribe the fortune-teller and falsely claim that Lu's daughter's birth year is unfavorable to my parents-in-law; shows a manipulative plan.
  • 吾以孝义谏亲，誓死不娶 - I will advise my parents with filial piety and swear never to marry; expresses a moral stance.
  • 父母素怜我，必毁前盟 - My parents have always loved me, and they will surely break the previous engagement; reflects the parents' influence.
  • 然后往就令狐不难矣 - Then it will not be difficult to go to Linghu; indicates the next step in his plan.
  • 筹思已定，归家以雨泞为词，云宿于姑所，父母亦不之疑 - After thinking it through, he returned home and used rain and mud as an excuse, saying he stayed at his aunt's house, and his parents did not suspect him; shows a deceptive tactic.
  • 翌日遂行其计，邑中知命者，咸私其金 - The next day, he carried out his plan, and the fortune-tellers in the town all accepted his bribes; indicates the success of his plan.
  • 父因子媳俱长，果思择吉竣其事 - My father, seeing that both my fiancée and I are grown, indeed thought to choose a good day to finalize the marriage; reflects the parents' intentions.
  • 宝知之，亦请随往 - Bao knew this and also asked to go along; shows his involvement.
  • 凡过数肆，皆攒眉曰 - Whenever he passed several shops, they all frowned and said; indicates the reactions of others.
  • 谁令君缔此盟者？妇入而君之伉俪俱殆矣 - Who made you make this promise? If the wife enters, your marriage will be in danger; expresses concern and disapproval.
  • 宝之父乃大骇 - Bao's father was greatly shocked; shows the impact of the fortune-tellers' warnings.
  • 盖闻女美，急于遣冰，其初故未卜之也 - Because he heard that the girl was beautiful, he was eager to send the matchmaker, but he did not initially know her fate; reflects the parents' initial eagerness.
  • 然以成言难毁，强之定期而归 - But because the promise was hard to break, he forced them to set a date and returned home; shows the parents' reluctance to change their decision.
  • 至暮，宝忽涕泣于母前曰 - At dusk, Bao suddenly wept in front of his mother and said; expresses his emotional state.
  • 生儿授室，虽出罔极之恩，实以尽奉养之道 - My son, you gave me a wife, although it was a boundless favor, it was actually to fulfill the duty of filial piety; reflects the parents' perspective on the marriage.

  • 令狐 - Refers to Linghu, another character in the story.
  • 诹日 - Refers to choosing a favorable date for a wedding.
  • 日者 - Refers to fortune-tellers or astrologers.
  • 年庚 - Refers to the birth year and month of a person.
  • 翁姑 - Refers to the parents-in-law.
  • 孝义 - Refers to filial piety and righteousness.
  • 遣冰 - Refers to sending a matchmaker to arrange a marriage.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 新妇 - Refers to a newly married woman, specifically in the context of a marriage.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 益得 - Suggests becoming more successful or gaining more (in terms of wealth, status, or knowledge).
  • 屈指浃旬 - Literally means 'counting on one's fingers for ten days,' implying a sense of urgency or anticipation.
  • 虑女有失 - Means 'worrying about the loss of the girl,' where '虑' means to worry or be concerned, and '女有失' could mean the girl might be lost or something unfortunate might happen to her.
  • 践言 - Means 'to fulfill a promise' or 'to keep one's word.'
  • 归告父母 - Means 'to return and inform one's parents.'
  • 劝使委禽 - Ambiguous phrase; '劝使' means 'to persuade' or 'to urge,' and '委禽' can mean 'to present a gift of poultry' as part of a marriage proposal or 'to entrust a task' or 'to give a task.'
  • 庶几周匝 - Means 'perhaps' or 'hopefully' in the sense of achieving completeness or thoroughness.
  • 独行以往 - Means 'to go alone' or 'to proceed alone.'
  • 跋履无难 - Means 'to travel without difficulty' or 'to walk without hindrance.'
  • 甫至村中 - Means 'just arrived at the village.'
  • 遭杖者于道 - Means 'encountered someone with a stick on the road.'
  • 欢然握手 - Means 'to greet joyfully by shaking hands.'
  • 延之至家 - Means 'to invite him to one's home.'

  • 退休于此有年矣 - Means 'I have retired here for many years.'

  • 系邻人寄养者 - Means 'she was a neighbor's child being raised by them,' indicating her status as a foster child.
  • 乃父远宦不能携 - Means 'her father was far away on official business and could not bring her along,' explaining the reason for her being raised by neighbors.
  • 故以之见属 - Means 'so he entrusted her to us,' indicating the formal arrangement.
  • 聘嫁亦皆在予也 - Means 'her marriage arrangements were also entrusted to me,' emphasizing the mother-in-law's role in her future.

  • 欲求为子妇 - Means 'to request her as a daughter-in-law,' expressing her desire.
  • 姑佯笑曰 - Means 'the mother-in-law feigned a laugh and said,' indicating a playful or evasive response.
